    Baptiste Gregorutti

    Head of Data Science at SCAI – Sorbonne Center for AI. Research engineer, educator, and team lead.

    • Paris, France
    • SCAI – Sorbonne Université
    • Email
    • GitHub
    • LinkedIn

    Publications

    Research output from my time at Safety Line (2011–2020), in collaboration with INRIA, LIP6 (Sorbonne), and IFSTTAR. All work is grounded in real flight data and aviation applications.

    Procédé d’analyse d’un vol d’un aéronef pour évaluer l’exposition à un risque donné dudit vol

    Published in Patent, 2019

    Patent — Method for analysing an aircraft flight to assess exposure to a given risk. Filed during the Safety Line period, arising from R&D work on automated flight risk scoring using large-scale flight data recorder processing and machine learning.

    Recommended citation: Gregorutti, B. et al. Patent — Procédé d'analyse d'un vol d'un aéronef pour évaluer l'exposition à un risque donné dudit vol. Safety Line.
    Download Paper

    Block sparse linear models for learning structured dynamical systems in aeronautics

    Published in Journal / Conference (add details), 2018

    Identification of structured dynamical systems from large-scale flight data using block sparse linear modelling. Application to aircraft trajectory analysis and system identification from flight data recorder streams.

    Recommended citation: Gregorutti, B. et al. (2018). Block sparse linear models for learning structured dynamical systems in aeronautics.
    Download Paper

    Gaussian Mixture Penalty for Trajectory Optimization Problems

    Published in Journal / Conference (add details), 2017

    Trajectory optimisation under distributional constraints using Gaussian mixture penalties. Applied to aircraft climb profile optimisation to balance fuel efficiency with operational constraints.

    Recommended citation: Gregorutti, B. et al. (2017). Gaussian Mixture Penalty for Trajectory Optimization Problems.
    Download Paper

    Correlation and variable importance in random forests

    Published in Statistics and Computing, 2017

    Theoretical and empirical analysis of variable importance measures in random forests in the presence of correlated predictors. Studies the bias introduced by correlation on standard importance scores and proposes corrections.

    Recommended citation: Gregorutti, B., Michel, B., Saint-Pierre, P. (2017). Correlation and variable importance in random forests. Statistics and Computing.
    Download Paper

    Grouped variable importance with random forests and application to multivariate functional data analysis

    Published in Journal / Conference (add details), 2015

    Extension of variable importance measures to grouped variables in random forests. Application to multivariate functional data from flight data recorders, where variables are naturally grouped by physical sensor type. Enables identification of the most relevant sensor groups for a given prediction task.

    Recommended citation: Gregorutti, B., Michel, B., Saint-Pierre, P. (2015). Grouped variable importance with random forests and application to multivariate functional data analysis.
    Download Paper

    Curve clustering based on second order information: application to bad runway condition detection

    Published in Journal / Conference (add details), 2014

    Clustering of functional data (curves) using second-order statistics. Applied to runway condition monitoring from flight data recorder streams — detecting degraded surface conditions from aircraft behaviour during landing roll.

    Recommended citation: Gregorutti, B. et al. (2014). Curve clustering based on second order information: application to bad runway condition detection.
    Download Paper
